articles.title: All the evidence revealed so far in Musk v. Altman
articles.summary: The Musk v. Altman trial reveals emails and documents showing Musk drafted OpenAI's mission and influenced its structure, while Nvidia provided computing resources and executives worried about Musk's control.

articles.title: Pentagon strikes classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, and Nvidia — but not Anthropic
articles.summary: The Pentagon signed classified AI deals with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, xAI, and Reflection, but excluded Anthropic citing supply-chain risks.

articles.title: Elon Musk had a bad week in court
articles.summary: Elon Musk's trial against OpenAI proceeded poorly during his testimony. He initiated the case claiming OpenAI stole a nonprofit and was the driving force behind the company, but evidence suggests he won't prevail.

articles.title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
articles.summary: The Academy has ruled that AI-generated actors and scripts are ineligible for Oscar nominations. This policy affects productions relying solely on artificial intelligence for creative work.

articles.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
articles.summary: AI-powered dictation apps enable hands-free voice input for emails, notes, and coding. These tools boost productivity by converting speech to text accurately.

articles.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
articles.summary: Replit's Amjad Masad discussed the company's future at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event, addressing whether Replit would sell given competitor Cursor's reported $60 billion acquisition talks with SpaceX.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with two million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but free open-source alternative Goose offers similar functionality locally without subscription fees or limitations.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt helped the startup attract engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: Ubuntu infrastructure has been down for more than a day
articles.summary: Ubuntu and Canonical servers have been offline for over 24 hours due to a sustained cross-border cyberattack, preventing OS updates and normal communications following a major vulnerability disclosure.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
articles.summary: A critical unpatched Linux vulnerability granting root access was publicly exploited, catching defenders unprepared. Despite kernel patches in multiple versions, most distributions hadn't incorporated fixes when exploit code was released.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx fell victim to multiple supply-chain attacks over six weeks, including a Trivy scanner compromise and direct GitHub breach that delivered malware to customers seeking credentials.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming CEO Sam Altman deceived him into funding the company. He admitted xAI uses OpenAI's models for training while warning about AI existential risks.

articles.title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
articles.summary: AI is expanding cybersecurity attack surfaces and adding complexity that legacy security approaches cannot adequately address. Security must be fundamentally rethought with AI integration at its core rather than added as an afterthought.

articles.title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
articles.summary: Companies are gaining control of their data to customize AI for their specific needs while maintaining data quality and safety. AI factories enable organizations and governments to scale operations sustainably while prioritizing data governance and control.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: AI evaluation has emerged as a major bottleneck in AI development, replacing compute as the limiting factor. Organizations now struggle with costly and time-consuming assessment processes needed to validate model performance.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advanced language models built with improved architecture and training methods. They demonstrate enhanced performance across various tasks through optimized design and computational efficiency.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra has been added as an inference provider on Hugging Face, enabling developers to access AI models through DeepInfra's infrastructure.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its potential applications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation. Companies like Hot Wheels use these solutions to streamline workflows and generate imaginative marketing content.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming technologies. The initiative aims to advance agricultural innovation through accessible AI and digital tools.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates its 20th anniversary, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages today. The company shares fun facts highlighting the service's growth and development.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. This vibe coding course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing your home and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This advancement explores how AI can support clinicians in patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling local and global updates, improving fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training across multiple nodes.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text accuracy and screenshot generation. The episode discusses this alongside Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 model updates.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: Episode discusses OpenAI's new GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher prices, Mistral's open-sourced Small 4 model family, and developments in attention mechanisms.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 uses generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time, boosting photorealism. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: SAP: How enterprise AI governance secures profit margins
articles.summary: Enterprise AI governance ensures profit margins by replacing statistical approximations with deterministic control. Organizations must prioritize precision and accuracy in production environments, where the difference between 90% and 100% accuracy is existential.

articles.title: Per-token AI charges come to GitHub Copilot
articles.summary: GitHub Copilot shifts from flat-rate subscriptions to per-token pricing starting June 1, 2026, aligning with standard LLM API pricing models where users pay based on actual token usage.

articles.title: What LG and NVIDIA’s talks reveal about the future of physical AI
articles.summary: LG and NVIDIA are exploring partnerships in physical AI, data centers, and mobility, revealing significant infrastructure and capital requirements needed to deploy autonomous systems beyond simulation.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when these failures occur, addressing key challenges in collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code AI assistant leaks sensitive API keys to public code repositories, creating security vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeover attacks, creating a significant supply chain security risk.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces generalization ability. Concise training weakens models' capacity to explore alternative solutions for complex problems.

articles.title: What is Tokenization Drift and How to Fix It?
articles.summary: Tokenization drift occurs when minor formatting changes in text input create different token sequences, causing unpredictable model behavior shifts. Models learn specific formatting patterns during training, so deviations push inputs outside their familiar distribution.

articles.title: Mistral AI Launches Remote Agents in Vibe and Mistral Medium 3.5 with 77.6% SWE-Bench Verified Score
articles.summary: Mistral AI launched remote agents in Vibe and introduced Mistral Medium 3.5, a 128B model achieving 77.6% on SWE-Bench. The upgrades enhance their coding agent ecosystem for open-source development.

articles.title: Build a Multi-Agent AI Workflow for Biological Network Modeling, Protein Interactions, Metabolism, and Cell Signaling Simulation
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building a multi-agent AI workflow for biological systems modeling, including gene regulation, protein interactions, metabolism, and cell signaling simulation in Google Colab using an OpenAI model as principal investigator.

articles.title: Why I own 4 different pairs of headphones, and how I effectively use each one
articles.summary: The article explores why owning multiple headphone pairs is practical, showcasing four versatile options that nearly achieve all-in-one functionality for different users and use cases.

articles.title: I enabled Data Saver mode on my Android phone to avoid overcharges - and it's a big relief
articles.summary: Android's Data Saver mode helps users manage mobile data consumption and prevent exceeding their plan limits, providing relief from potential overcharges.

articles.title: T-Mobile is giving 5G customers Hulu and Netflix subscriptions, free - here's how to get them
articles.summary: T-Mobile adds complimentary Hulu and Netflix subscriptions (ad-supported) to Experience Beyond and Go5G Next plans for eligible customers.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, an algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines used in RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables Python developers to build intelligent AI agents efficiently. The framework simplifies agent development with built-in validation and structured data handling.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: This guide teaches developers how to effectively engineer context for AI agents to improve their performance and reliability. It covers best practices for structuring prompts and information to maximize agent capabilities.

articles.title: Which Regularizer Should You Actually Use? Lessons from 134,400 Simulations
articles.summary: Study of 134,400 simulations provides practitioners with a decision framework for choosing between Ridge, Lasso, and ElasticNet regularizers based on three computable quantities before model fitting.

articles.title: How a 2021 Quantization Algorithm Quietly Outperforms Its 2026 Successor
articles.summary: A 2021 quantization algorithm outperforms its 2026 successor, with a single scale parameter determining accuracy in rotation-based vector quantization.

articles.title: How to Get Hired in the AI Era
articles.summary: The article discusses what employers seek when hiring junior AI professionals, highlighting qualities that make candidates stand out in the competitive AI job market.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw, an open-source AI agent project, gained massive traction by early 2026, crossing 100,000 GitHub stars and enabling organizations to build specialized AI systems on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: NVIDIA GeForce NOW adds 16 games this May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light. The platform expands RTX 5080 power to its Install-to-Play library for instant streaming.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal AI model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities. This single-system approach enables 9x more efficient AI agents with faster processing and improved reasoning across multiple data types.

articles.title: AWS Transform now automates BI migration to Amazon Quick in days
articles.summary: AWS Transform automates business intelligence migration to Amazon QuickSight, reducing timelines from months to days while preserving dashboards, calculated fields, and security rules.

articles.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning with LLM-as-a-judge
articles.summary: Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) uses automated reward signals to align LLMs efficiently, replacing costly manual labeling. Two approaches—RLVR with verifiable code rewards and RLAIF with LLM-as-judge evaluation—guide models toward better outputs.

articles.title: AWS Generative AI Model Agility Solution: A comprehensive guide to migrating LLMs for generative AI production
articles.summary: AWS provides a comprehensive guide for migrating large language models in production environments, emphasizing structured approaches to maintain model agility while minimizing disruption. The solution requires balancing generality with specificity, enabling fair LLM comparisons, and incorporating automated, scalable processes.

articles.title: Speed Up Unreal Engine NNE Inference with NVIDIA TensorRT for RTX Runtime
articles.summary: NVIDIA TensorRT now accelerates neural network inference in Unreal Engine's NNE for RTX, enabling faster image quality improvements and real-time graphics rendering through techniques like super resolution and denoising.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and enhanced Super Resolution for game developers integrating with Unreal Engine 5 and RTX technology.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: ComfyUI is an open-source, node-based tool that uses generative AI to automate creative workflows, compressing manual tasks into repeatable pipelines for visualization teams.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in XR development.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient shaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, aiming to make AI systems more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on designing imaging systems using information theory to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. Uses noisy measurements and noise models to quantify imaging system performance.
